nts1.d: Veteran NTS host Hampus brings you Doing Time: a genre-agnostic monthly trip through quality records from around the world’s past and present, featuring regular appearances from musical friends and family.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM
nts1.d: PAM, previously host of beloved NTS show Okonkole Y Trompa, joins you for a dawn chorus every Thursday. Traversing obscure rarities, globetrotting vintage pop and beyond.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.
Low, slow and sleazy sounds with babyschön, in the studio for the early afternoon shift…

Shanti is a veteran and expert in four to the floor dance music - tune in once a month for a considered two-hour selection of the best house and techno around, transmitting direct from the NTS studio.
Zurich-based Jenny Cara brings the heat with a blend of cheerful and hard-hitting house, creating a space that celebrates community, history, and pure energy.
Monthly missives from a London club selector par excellence - expect anything from finger-on-the-pulse UK rap, to deep house and techno records, to jungle & UK funky.

Andalusian, Berlin-based producer and DJ Chico Blanco drops in monthly with a mix of his top picks — shades of house, underground Spanish pop, dreamy romantic melodies, plus guest spots from longtime friends sharing their favourites.
From the host: "Chico Blanco invites Juguete"
International party starters from Huntleys + Palmers label head.
Hip-hop scholar, esteemed illustrator and all round head Ted Draws holds down a killer 2 hour monthly slot on Thursday afternoons.

nts1.n: THE TIM & BARRY SHOW w/ Tim b2b DJ spinn & Traxman
RADIO SUPER W/ ROSA PISTOLA - Tributo a EROS
THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO
The NTS Breakfast Show w/ TASH LC
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SCARY THINGS

nts2.d: This episode is influenced by King's trip to Hawaii. Field recordings and found sounds all from the island. All music composed and written exclusively by King Britt for Transmissions. Special Guest: Moksha Black on electronics

Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "Step inside the sonic laboratory of Soviet Ukraine’s Kyivnaukfilm (Eng: Kyiv Science Film, active years 1941-1993) studio — a workshop where composers and sound designers crafted scores for documentary, science, education, and animation films. From eerie synth textures to inventive tape collages, these rare recordings (1967–1990) reveal a secret world of avant-garde music created under censorship yet brimming with experiments."

nts2.n: The Uline Catalog w/ Calvin LeCompte (R)
nts2.d: Leaf through songwriter Calvin LeCompte’s monthly selection via The Uline Catalog…
Westward Ho! - Ashley Wales playing all things classical with a special focus on music from the 20th and 21st century.
Occult tapes plundered by two low level scavengers: the finest dungeon synth, medieval dark ambient & black metal.

Chicago-based archival record label, The Numero Group, host a deep-digging once a month show. Expect comprehensive round ups from their crates, picking out the best old music and reissues, as well as some jubilant nerdy record chat.

Danny Fitzgerald joins us for an hour of tropical oldies from the Caribbean, Latin America, West Africa, and beyond.
From the host: "Includes special listener requests to commemorate the final edition of the Rum Punch show."
Tom Furse is a musician and compiler - searching out the spaces in between genres - tune in for all things wonky; an education in spacious sound and dusted soul.

Desire paths are formed by repeated deviation from official routes.
Artist/producer Laura Groves explores private roads and dreamscapes with a series of mixtapes featuring demos, synth sketches, collaborations, pop classics, field recordings and soundtracks.

nts2.n: HEAVEN AND EARTH MAGIC W/ JOCELYN ROMO (R)
nts2.d: Exploring inner and outer worlds, cosmic and pastoral sounds, and the intersection between reality and dreams once a month with Jocelyn Romo's mix of international and private press folk, instrumental guitar, dream country, ambient, new age, kosmiche, psych and beyond
Regular hours from the D.C. archivists and record label, famed for uncovering + documenting folk and global traditional music.

Where the wall meets the floor, Temporal Cove is a body of sound exploring the ethereal and ephemeral. Smilegoth dives into shoegaze, post-rock, house and more to articulate a flow of reverberations that can be felt on every surface.
From the host: "smilegoth invites one of her favorite bands, Model/Actriz for a 1 hour guest mix on Temporal Cove. Ruben and Jack from Model/Actriz dig through their favorite live(ish) drum recordings."

nts2.n: Brown Acid Trippin' w/ Lance Barresi & DJ Tara Dactyl
CDMX Tapes w/ BRVVS & BASY TROPIKALNE

subtle.d: Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: George Jensen and Heavy Hymns - Quietudes
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
In Sheep's Clothing – World of Sound
Dustin Wong – Why Did Why Did Why Did

rinsefr.j: : Music For The Soul #16 (Better Days w/ Matteo)
